finding.contract_claim: Parameter `plumbing_result` is declared in the signature of build_scorecard(config, analysis_results, junk_results=None, dead_code_results=None, plumbing_result=None) (signature at lines 106-113) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: No call site ever passes `plumbing_result`: the only caller, run_audit in src/osoji/audit.py, invokes build_scorecard with config, analysis_results and junk_results only, so the parameter always takes its default None and the branches gated on it never execute.

finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: The parameter plumbing_result is never passed by any caller. The only call site (run_audit in audit.py) does not supply it, so it always resolves to None and any branches gated on it are permanently dead. The export surface check shows build_scorecard is not exported, so the 'backward compat' comment at L110 covers both parameters but lacks an identifiable external consumer. Significance: Dead parameter with misleading compat comment. Actionability: Remove it along with its dead branches.
finding.suggested_fix: Remove the plumbing_result parameter from build_scorecard (and its gated branches) if no external callers depend on it.
